video_title: ‘The Drowned City’ Review: Lush Prose or Murky Plot?
author: Leena Hart
summary: An evocative maritime gothic that balances atmosphere with character interiority. [SPOILER] A late-reveal lineage twist is mentioned. [/SPOILER] The reviewer praises the confident pacing and a striking final image.
key_quotes: Its tide charts the grief we refuse to map.
Every lantern lit is a memory refusing to drown.
Hart’s restraint makes the revelations feel tidal rather than torrential.
